enonce.texte: Choose the correct modal verb to complete each sentence. must / mustn't / have to / don't have to / should / shouldn't / ought to

questions.texte: Complete the sentences with the appropriate modal verb.

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ (obligation) wear a seatbelt when you drive.
sousQuestions.reponse: must / have to

sousQuestions.texte: She _______ (advice) eat more fruits and vegetables.
sousQuestions.reponse: should / ought to

sousQuestions.texte: Students _______ (prohibition) cheat during exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: mustn't

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ (no obligation) pay for this service; it's free.
sousQuestions.reponse: don't have to

sousQuestions.texte: We _______ (strong advice) leave now if we don't want to be late.
sousQuestions.reponse: ought to / should

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with must or have to. Pay attention to whether the obligation is internal (personal) or external.

questions.texte: Choose the correct form to express the obligation.

sousQuestions.texte: I _______ (personal decision) study harder to pass my exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: must (internal obligation / personal decision)

sousQuestions.texte: You _______ (external rule) wear a uniform at school.
sousQuestions.reponse: have to (external obligation / rule)

sousQuestions.texte: The doctor said I _______ (external) take this medicine twice a day.
sousQuestions.reponse: have to (external obligation from the doctor)

sousQuestions.texte: I _______ (personal feeling) call my parents; I miss them.
sousQuestions.reponse: must (internal feeling / personal obligation)

sousQuestions.texte: In Cameroon, citizens _______ (law) have a national ID card.
sousQuestions.reponse: have to (legal obligation / law)

enonce.texte: Give advice using should / shouldn't / ought to for each situation.

questions.texte: Write a piece of advice for each situation.

sousQuestions.texte: My friend is always tired in class.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to go to bed earlier.

sousQuestions.texte: My brother wants to improve his English.
sousQuestions.reponse: He should / ought to practice speaking every day.

sousQuestions.texte: I have a headache.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to take some medicine and rest.

sousQuestions.texte: My sister spends too much time on her phone.
sousQuestions.reponse: She shouldn't spend so much time on her phone. She should / ought to read books instead.

sousQuestions.texte: I feel stressed about my upcoming exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to organize your time well and take regular breaks.

enonce.texte: Read each situation and give an appropriate response using modal verbs of obligation or advice.

questions.texte: Respond to each situation using must, have to, should, ought to, don't have to, mustn't.

sousQuestions.texte: You are a school principal. A student asks if they can use their phone in class. (Give a rule)
sousQuestions.reponse: You mustn't use your phone in class. It is against the school rules. / You have to turn off your phone during class.

sousQuestions.texte: Your friend wants to apply for a scholarship but doesn't know where to start. (Give advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to visit the scholarship office first. You should also prepare your documents carefully.

sousQuestions.texte: You are a doctor. A patient asks what they should do to stay healthy. (Give advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to eat a balanced diet and exercise regularly. You also mustn't smoke.

sousQuestions.texte: Your younger sibling wants to stay out late. You are the older sibling. (Give a rule and advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: You have to be home by 8 pm. You shouldn't stay out too late; it's dangerous. You must call me if you're going to be late.

sousQuestions.texte: You are a teacher. A student asks if they can be absent from class. (Give a rule and advice)
sousQuestions.reponse: You must bring a medical certificate if you are absent. You should also inform the school administration. You don't have to come if you are sick, but you have to let us know.

enonce.texte: Translate the following sentences from French to English using appropriate modal verbs.

sousQuestions.texte: Tu dois respecter le code de la route.
sousQuestions.reponse: You must / have to obey the traffic rules.

sousQuestions.texte: Tu ne devrais pas manger trop de sucre.
sousQuestions.reponse: You shouldn't / ought not to eat too much sugar.

sousQuestions.texte: Nous devons protéger l'environnement.
sousQuestions.reponse: We must / have to protect the environment.

sousQuestions.texte: Vous devez vous inscrire avant la date limite.
sousQuestions.reponse: You have to / must register before the deadline.

sousQuestions.texte: Il faudrait que tu parles à ton professeur.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should / ought to talk to your teacher.

enonce.texte: You are the discipline master at a secondary school. A group of students is not following the school rules. Write a short dialogue showing how you explain the obligations and give advice. Use must, have to, should, ought to, mustn't, don't have to.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between the discipline master and a student.
questions.reponse:
Discipline Master (DM): Good morning. Do you know why I called you?
Student (S): No, sir.
DM: You were using your phone during class. You mustn't use phones during lessons. It's against the school rules.
S: I'm sorry, sir. I didn't know.
DM: You have to read the school regulations. All students have to follow them.
S: I understand, sir.
DM: You should also be more attentive in class. If you have questions, you should ask the teacher. You don't have to use your phone to find answers.
S: Yes, sir. I will do better.
DM: You ought to set a good example for other students. And remember, you must inform the school if you have any problems. You can come to my office anytime.
S: Thank you, sir. I promise to follow the rules.

enonce.texte: Each sentence contains one mistake. Identify and correct it.

questions.texte: Correct the mistakes in the following sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: He must to study harder for his exams.
sousQuestions.reponse: He must study harder for his exams. (Correct: 'to' is not needed after 'must'.)

sousQuestions.texte: You don't must use your phone in the library.
sousQuestions.reponse: You mustn't use your phone in the library. / You don't have to use your phone in the library. (Correct: 'must' cannot be used with 'don't'.)

sousQuestions.texte: She ought to works harder.
sousQuestions.reponse: She ought to work harder. (Correct: after 'ought to' we use the base form of the verb.)

sousQuestions.texte: We have to not be late for the meeting.
sousQuestions.reponse: We mustn't be late for the meeting. / We don't have to be late? (Correct: the negative of 'have to' is 'don't have to'.)

sousQuestions.texte: You should to eat more vegetables.
sousQuestions.reponse: You should eat more vegetables. (Correct: 'to' is not needed after 'should'.)

enonce.texte: You are an advice columnist. Read the letters from readers and write a response using appropriate modal verbs (must, have to, should, ought to, mustn't, don't have to).

questions.texte: Write a response to each letter.

sousQuestions.texte: Letter 1: "I want to get better grades but I don't know how. What should I do?"
sousQuestions.reponse:
Dear Student,

You should create a study schedule and stick to it. You ought to review your lessons every day. You mustn't procrastinate or wait until the last minute to study. You have to ask your teachers for help if you don't understand something. You don't have to study all day; you should take regular breaks to rest. Remember, you must believe in yourself!

Good luck!

sousQuestions.texte: Letter 2: "I have a lot of stress because of exams. What can I do?"
sousQuestions.reponse:
Dear Student,

You shouldn't worry too much; it's normal to feel stressed. You ought to practice relaxation techniques like deep breathing. You must eat healthy food and get enough sleep. You should also talk to someone you trust about your feelings. You don't have to be perfect; just do your best. You have to take care of your mental health!

Take care.

sousQuestions.texte: Letter 3: "My friends want me to skip school, but I'm scared. What should I do?"
sousQuestions.reponse:
Dear Student,

You mustn't skip school. It is against the rules and it will hurt your grades. You should tell your friends that you don't want to skip. You ought to find better friends who respect your choices. You have to be responsible and think about your future. If you're scared, you should talk to a teacher or a trusted adult. You don't have to do what your friends want; make your own decisions.

Stay strong!
